title: Interpret How OptiSweep Differs From Traditional and Belted Bagging Models
summary: Use the source comparison to identify the documented operating characteristics that distinguish OptiSweep from traditional small sort and belted ACB bagging approaches.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing a system description, training discussion, or observed process and you need to classify it against the source-described models: traditional small sort bagging, belted ACB, or FORTNA's OptiSweep solution.
not_for: Do not use for detailed operational control of OptiSweep equipment.
Do not use to infer additional OptiSweep operating details beyond the comparison points stated in the source.
Do not use when the observed system characteristics do not align with any of the source-described models.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the source comparison and match observed or described system characteristics to the source-described model.

safety_notes: This is a reference and interpretation procedure derived from training content, not a physical operating sequence.
Do not infer unsupported controls, process steps, or system behavior beyond what is stated in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: System description or observed process characteristics to compare

access_required: Access to the training source comparison content for training_video_day1 segment 0004
system_preconditions: A system description, observed process description, or discussion points are available for comparison against the source models

visual_references.description: Comparison slide showing traditional small sort bagging, belted ACB, and FORTNA's AGV/tipper-based OptiSweep solution.

steps.title: Review the source comparison
steps.purpose: Establish the source-defined comparison points before classifying a system description.
steps.instruction: Review the source comparison between the traditional system, the belted ACB solution, and FORTNA's solution.
steps.expected_result: The comparison categories and their distinguishing characteristics are visible and available for reference.
steps.healthy_condition: The reviewer can see the three compared models and the source-stated characteristics for each.
steps.failure_condition: The comparison slide or transcript is unavailable, incomplete, or unclear enough that the reviewer cannot reliably compare models.

actions.label: Review comparison source
actions.exact_action: Review the source comparison between the traditional system, the belted ACB solution, and FORTNA's solution.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The comparison slide titled around traditional vs FORTNA's solution, showing traditional small sort bagging, belted ACB, and FORTNA's AGV/tipper-based solution.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the source comparison cannot be accessed or does not provide enough detail to distinguish the models.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide compares a traditional small sort bagging process, a belted ACB solution, and Fortna's automated Consolidated Bagging solution using AGVs and tippers.

steps.title: Check for traditional small sort bagging characteristics
steps.purpose: Determine whether the described process matches the traditional operator-intensive model from the source.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the described flow depends on operators manually closing destinations, printing and applying labels, zipping bags, and placing bags on a takeaway belt for 20-25 destinations.
steps.expected_result: You can determine whether the described process matches the traditional small sort bagging model.
steps.healthy_condition: The described process clearly includes or excludes the source-stated traditional characteristics: operators assigned about 20-25 destinations, parcels falling into bags, and manual full-bag handling steps.
steps.failure_condition: The description is too incomplete or ambiguous to determine whether the traditional model applies.

actions.label: Compare against traditional model
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the described flow depends on operators manually closing destinations, printing and applying labels, zipping bags, and placing bags on a takeaway belt for 20-25 destinations.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional small sort bagging portion of the comparison slide, including operator assignment to about 20-25 destinations and manual bag handling steps.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed or described process does not clearly confirm or rule out the traditional characteristics.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Operators manage about 20-25 destinations on a sorter, parcels fall into bags, and full bags require manual close, label, zip, takeaway belt placement, and chute reopening.

In a traditional small sort system, operators are assigned sections of destinations (20-25) on a sorter; when a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

steps.title: Check for belted ACB characteristics
steps.purpose: Determine whether the described process matches the conveyor-based belted ACB model from the source.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the described flow uses a full destination bin dropped onto a takeaway conveyor and conveyed as a slug down a chute into a bag station.
steps.expected_result: You can determine whether the described process matches the belted ACB model.
steps.healthy_condition: The described process clearly includes or excludes the source-stated belted ACB characteristic of a full destination bin dropping to a takeaway conveyor and moving as a slug to a bag station.
steps.failure_condition: The description is too incomplete or ambiguous to determine whether the belted ACB model applies.

actions.label: Compare against belted ACB model
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the described flow uses a full destination bin dropped onto a takeaway conveyor and conveyed as a slug down a chute into a bag station.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Belted ACB portion of the comparison slide describing a full destination bin dropped onto a takeaway conveyor and conveyed as a slug to a bag station.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed or described process does not clearly confirm or rule out the belted ACB characteristics.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Full destination bins drop to a takeaway conveyor, packages move as a slug through a chute to a bag station.
In a Belted ACB solution when a destination is full the bin is dropped on a takeaway conveyor that conveys the packages as a slug down a chute into a bag station.

steps.title: Check for OptiSweep characteristics
steps.purpose: Determine whether the described process matches the source-defined OptiSweep model.
steps.instruction: Identify whether the described solution is characterized as using AGVs and tippers for a flexible, scalable, modular, and adaptive bagging system.
steps.expected_result: You can determine whether the described process matches the source-defined OptiSweep approach.
steps.healthy_condition: The described solution aligns with the source characterization of OptiSweep as an automated consolidated bagging solution using AGVs and tippers, with flexible, scalable, modular, and adaptive design intent and little modification to existing sorters.
steps.failure_condition: The description does not clearly include the source-stated OptiSweep characteristics or conflicts with them.

actions.label: Compare against OptiSweep model
actions.exact_action: Identify whether the described solution is characterized as using AGVs and tippers for a flexible, scalable, modular, and adaptive bagging system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: FORTNA solution portion of the comparison slide describing AGVs and tippers and the flexible, scalable, modular, and adaptive design.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed system characteristics do not align with any of the source-described models.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: FORTNA automated Consolidated Bagging solution using AGVs and tippers for a flexible, scalable, modular, and adaptive bagging system with little modification to existing sorters.
AGVs and tippers support scalable solutions and tailored maps for existing building sorters with minimal modification.
FORTNA developed an automated Consolidated Bagging (ACB) solution utilizing AGVs and tippers to create a flexible and scalable bagging system.

FORTNA wanted a more modular and adaptive design that would need little modification to existing sorters; by utilizing AGVs and tippers we can scale solutions and tailor maps to existing building sorters.

steps.title: Record the matching model
steps.purpose: Document the classification result using only source-supported characteristics.
steps.instruction: Record which model matches the observed or discussed system description using only the source-provided characteristics.
steps.expected_result: A source-grounded classification is recorded as traditional small sort bagging, belted ACB, or OptiSweep.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded model is supported by one or more source-stated characteristics and does not rely on inferred details.
steps.failure_condition: No model can be matched from the available description, or the classification depends on unsupported assumptions.

actions.label: Document source-grounded classification
actions.exact_action: Record which model matches the observed or discussed system description using only the source-provided characteristics.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the comparison slide as the basis for the final model classification.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed system characteristics do not align with any of the source-described models.
Stop if classification would require inferring additional OptiSweep operating details beyond the comparison points stated in the source.

source_evidence.candidate_note: Do not infer additional OptiSweep operating details beyond the comparison points stated in the source.

success_criteria: The user can distinguish the documented characteristics of OptiSweep from the traditional small sort and belted ACB models described in the source.
A model classification is recorded using only source-provided characteristics.
healthy_conditions: Traditional model is identified only when the description includes operator-managed destinations and manual bag close, label, zip, takeaway belt placement, and chute reopening behavior.
Belted ACB model is identified only when the description includes a full destination bin dropping onto a takeaway conveyor and moving as a slug to a bag station.
OptiSweep is identified only when the description aligns with AGVs and tippers and the source-stated flexible, scalable, modular, and adaptive design intent.
failure_conditions: The source comparison cannot be reviewed.
The observed or discussed system description is too incomplete or ambiguous to match a source-described model.
The observed system characteristics do not align with any of the source-described models.
Classification would require unsupported inference beyond the source comparison.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed system characteristics do not align with any of the source-described models.
Escalate if the available description is insufficient to distinguish between traditional small sort bagging, belted ACB, and OptiSweep.
Do not extend the classification with additional operating details not stated in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary comparison slide used throughout this reference procedure.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: FORTNA developed an automated Consolidated Bagging (ACB) solution utilizing AGVs and tippers to create a flexible and scalable bagging system.

FORTNA wanted a more modular and adaptive design that would need little modification to existing sorters; by utilizing AGVs and tippers we can scale solutions and tailor maps to existing building sorters.

missing_details: The source does not provide a formal decision table or explicit tie-breaker logic when characteristics appear mixed.
The source does not define a required recording system or output format for documenting the classification.
The source does not provide estimated completion time.
The source does not specify additional supporting roles beyond the interpretive L1_support role selected in the candidate.
